Purpose: Increase the number of market rate properties in downtown through construction, rehabilitation, and renovation
Mechanism: Investment Tax Credit for up to 10% of development costs and Local Property Tax Exemption in specific zones in and around downtown

Purpose: Increase the number of very low-, low-, and moderate-income housing units
Mechanism: Increase Developer Limit on market rate housing for each affordable unit built.

Program Title: High Performance Market-Rate Rental Housing Tax Credit
Purpose: Encourage development of new, renovated, or converted market-rate apartments
Mechanism: Tax Credit, decreasing over time, for 10 or 15 years ( for citywide vs targeted areas) based on the increase in the real property's value

Purpose: Expand diversity of housing in the CCHIP Zone by converting or rehabilitating existing buildings, including mixed use buildings
Mechanism: Local Tax Exemption of 100% for up to 10 years if project started in first 5 years of program, negotiated rate for the last 15 years of the program
Other Aspects: 20 year zone for project; Length and amount of exemption project dependent

Program Title: J Van Sickle & Company Rural Rental Housing Development Model
Purpose: Create profitable market rate housing in rural communities
Mechanism: Decrease acquisition and development costs through Part Ownership in the project; Implement a New Management Model with zero cost

Purpose: Encourage development of new market-rate apartments

Program Title: Other Applicable Examples - Case Studies, Articles, etc.

Mechanism: Non-profit and for-profit developers collaborated to offset the loss in profits from the city's inclusionary housing ordinance  with market rate units
Other Aspects: Market rate/affordable housing mix (Mixed income housing)
Mixed use commercial and housing development

Mechanism: suggested: changes to housing Tax Credits that make areas with a housing shortage eligible.
Other Aspects: Article suggesting changes to tax credit programs to attract market rate housing to rural areas with housing shortages. Developers pass by rural communities because urban areas have higher market rates and are more profitable.

Mechanism: Tax Increment Finance District and Local Tax Funding
Tax Increment Finance district for promotion of below market units, can be incorporated into mixed income properties.
Loan via the city at 1% interest over 20 years
Other Aspects: Workforce housing - up to 140% state median income; money from the Iowa Housing Loan Program through the city
